title: Harden Beads sync for this JSONL-only Logseq garden
description: The repo now intentionally runs Beads in JSONL-only mode, but bd doctor still flags sync-branch and merge-driver setup gaps. Follow up by choosing the right git-backed sync approach for a Logseq garden, keeping the repo JSONL-only and avoiding coding-project assumptions like in-repo worktrees.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Confirm and preserve JSONL-only Beads operation for this repo; do not introduce a committed SQLite database workflow.
2. Decide and implement the repo's intended bd sync strategy for multi-device use, including whether to configure a sync branch.
3. Configure the necessary git integration for that strategy (for example merge driver and any recommended hooks), or document why a specific integration is intentionally skipped.
4. Verify the chosen workflow with the relevant bd commands in normal mode and record any repo-specific caveats for Logseq usage.

title: Finalize logseq-entity skill, beads tracking, and type initialization
description:
Current state:
- Uncommitted work exists under .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/ and .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md.
- These changes refactor the logseq-entity Rulesync skill so it prefers Logseq-native ontology pages ([[Logseq/Entity]] and [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]]) as the primary source of truth, with .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md as a fallback/bootstrap config.
- A new reference file exists at .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/references/logseq-entity-type-pages.md.
- The skill now assumes [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]] is the canonical entity-type page and that ontology plus instance-template guidance should live together there by default, with an optional dedicated [[Logseq/Template/Entity/<Type>/Page]] only when needed.
- The repo already has unrelated user changes in journals/2026_03_14.md; do not include or revert that file.

Why this issue exists:
- The previous session was interrupted before Beads tasks and commits were created.
- The user wants this work tracked in Beads and committed cleanly.
- The user also wants the skill to help the AI and the human jointly infer and define which entity types a given garden covers, likely as a phased RPI (research/plan/implement) workflow.

Next steps to continue:
1. Review the current uncommitted skill/config changes and confirm they still reflect the intended hybrid model.
2. Create a Beads task for the work already done on the logseq-entity skill and close it if appropriate once committed.
3. Add an explicit affordance in the skill for initializing entity types for a garden. This should likely include:
   - a user-facing workflow such as "initialize entity types for this garden"
   - an RPI-style phased process to research the garden, propose candidate entity types, and then create/update [[Logseq/Entity]] and [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]] pages
   - guidance for when to create or update Logseq template pages for entity instances
4. Create a follow-up Beads task for that type-initialization capability.
5. Make clean commits without touching journals/2026_03_14.md.

Files likely relevant:
- .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/SKILL.md
- .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/references/configuration-contract.md
- .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/references/entity-search-and-dedup.md
- .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/references/entity-inference-from-journal.md
- .rulesync/skills/logseq-entity/references/logseq-entity-type-pages.md
- .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md
- .beads/issues.jsonl

Validation already performed:
- rulesync dry-run previously succeeded after the hybrid-model refactor: npx -y rulesync generate --dry-run --targets claudecode --features skills
acceptance_criteria:
1. The current logseq-entity skill changes are reviewed and committed without including unrelated journal edits.
2. A Beads issue exists for the landed logseq-entity refactor work.
3. The skill gains an explicit affordance for initializing entity types for a garden, ideally via an RPI-style phased workflow.
4. A Beads follow-up issue exists for further work on type inference/initialization if any part remains open.
5. The resulting skill still passes a Rulesync dry run for skills generation.
notes: Created bd-12 for follow-up garden initialization work and landed the hybrid logseq-entity skill in commit 6053d53.

title: Initialize entity types for this garden
description: Use the logseq-entity skill's RPI workflow to research which entity types this knowledge garden actually covers, propose the initial ontology, and create or update [[Logseq/Entity]] plus the relevant [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]] pages.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Research identifies the strongest candidate entity types already present in the garden.
2. A concrete initialization plan exists for [[Logseq/Entity]] and the first supported [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]] pages.
3. Implementation creates or updates the approved ontology pages while keeping .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md as fallback/bootstrap support.
4. Any deferred or ambiguous types are documented for follow-up.
notes: Closed: registry (Logseq/Entity) and software-project type page created and refined; logseq-entity:: convention documented; deferred types noted. Follow-up: bd-14 (safari to find software-project pages, add frontmatter, revise type page from patterns).

title: Land hybrid logseq-entity Rulesync refactor
description: Land the current logseq-entity skill/config refactor that makes [[Logseq/Entity]] and [[Logseq/Entity/<Type>]] the primary source of truth, with .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md retained as fallback/bootstrap configuration.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The Rulesync skill and references clearly preserve the hybrid model.
2. The fallback/bootstrap role of .rulesync/config/logseq-entity.md is explicit.
3. The work is committed cleanly without unrelated journal edits.
4. Rulesync dry-run for skills generation passes.

title: Safari: find software-project pages, add logseq-entity::, revise type page from patterns
description: Follow-up to bd-12. Find pages in the garden that are software projects, add logseq-entity:: [[Logseq/Entity/software-project]] to their frontmatter, link them appropriately, and revise the Logseq/Entity/software-project page from patterns learned.

title: Import a project 'landing the plane' rule via Rulesync and generate Codex instructions
description: Add this project’s equivalent of 'landing the plane' into AI instructions as a Rulesync-managed rule, not as an ad-hoc direct edit in AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A Rulesync rule file is added/updated under .rulesync to encode the 'landing the plane' behavior for this project. 2) rulesync generate is run successfully. 3) Generated Codex-specific instruction files are updated by generation output. 4) Generated files and the Rulesync source rule are committed to git with a clear commit message. 5) No manual drift remains between generated outputs and Rulesync source.
